authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. federal construction-safety regulator. This standard defines safety programs, frequent inspections, and competent-person oversight that can block commissioning work and startup windows.

authoritative_sources.title: After You Get a Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DOB is an official authority having jurisdiction and its permit-holder guidance describes permit posting, approved-plan availability, required inspections, signoff, and permit closeout steps.
used_for: permit signoff routing and closure boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: DOB NOW: Inspections Guide: Construction Inspection Sign Off Prior to Occupancy Request
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: An official building-department inspection guide that names permit-entire status, floor readiness, objection resolution, supporting documents, and inspection scheduling rules before occupancy-related construction signoff.
used_for: inspection scheduling and occupancy-gate artifact expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Building Commissioning: The Process
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized public federal and industry building-guidance platform. This commissioning reference defines commissioning process phases, OPR, commissioning plans, issues logs, systems manuals, training, and hand-off expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Commissioning phase sequencing and turnover preparation

authoritative_sources.title: Roles and Responsibilities in the Commissioning Process
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This recognized public workflow reference clarifies owner, commissioning provider, contractor, subcontractor, TAB, controls, and supplier responsibilities, making it authoritative for ownership and handoff boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Commissioning Documents: Process, Contents, and Acceptance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This recognized public commissioning reference defines commissioning documents such as OPR, basis of design, commissioning plans, checklists, issues logs, functional test procedures, systems manuals, and final reports that govern evidence completeness and acceptance.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Quality Management for Contractors Student Study Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE's official contractor quality-management guide governs preconstruction coordination, submittals, three-phase control, daily quality-control reports, testing tracking, and deficiency management in public construction administration.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request scope, commissioning stage, work order, system, permit, inspection, site-access, and owner context before any routing or closure output is produced.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Site-readiness, safety, permit, and startup screening
workflow_stages.description: Check whether site access, approved plans, permit status, temporary utilities, startup readiness, pre-task safety documents, and work-area controls are present before commissioning work is handed off.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team commissioning coordination and ownership resolution
workflow_stages.description: Identify prior owner, next owner, duplicated ownership, schedule conflicts, and required artifact completeness across owner, commissioning provider, superintendent, project controls, controls, TAB, safety, permitting, inspection, and trade teams.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Testing, deficiency, and turnover blocker handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve open issues logs, unresolved deficiencies, failed or pending functional tests, incomplete training, systems-manual gaps, and occupancy or closeout gates as blockers until the correct owner and evidence set are explicit.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Authority-boundary escalation and closure confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate whenever the request drifts into licensed trade judgment, design interpretation, code interpretation, permit approval, inspection signoff, owner acceptance, or safety-critical signoff, and confirm closure only after destination-owner acceptance is explicit.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

artifacts: commissioning plans, owner's project requirements, basis of design references, and commissioning specifications
work orders, scopes of work, subcontractor records, startup forms, and service requests
issued permits, permit cards, approved plans, inspection requests, objection logs, and AHJ correspondence
prefunctional checklists, TAB reports, controls evidence, functional performance test procedures, witness records, and issues logs
look-ahead schedules, integrated commissioning schedules, milestone logs, owner maps, and dependency trackers
site logs, pre-task plans, safety observations, stop-work notices, temporary utility readiness notes, and area-access releases
training records, systems manuals, O&M manuals, warranties, as-builts, seasonal-testing plans, final commissioning reports, and turnover packages
systems_of_record: building automation or controls evidence repository
scheduling system or look-ahead planning board

allowed: Classify commissioning coordination requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing permit, site, inspection, schedule, safety, startup, testing, training, or closeout prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, systems-of-record links, and authoritative records required to continue safely.
Track duplicated ownership, stale handoffs, and dependency failures across commissioning, controls, TAB, permitting, inspection, field, safety, and owner teams.
Produce handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations when evidence is complete.
escalate: Permit status, inspection requirements, safety conditions, startup readiness, testing evidence, training completion, or closeout evidence are missing or materially conflict across sources of record.
The request requires permit approval, inspection signoff, owner acceptance, code interpretation, licensed trade judgment, engineering review, or safety signoff.
Public guidance, tenant policy, contract terms, or jurisdictional instructions materially conflict on the next step.
A stop-work condition, objection, failed test, unresolved issue, or turnover deficiency changes whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Authorize work to proceed without required permit, site-readiness, safety, startup, inspection, or testing prerequisites.
Approve permit signoff, inspection signoff, owner acceptance, code compliance, licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record decisions, or design interpretation.
Close a commissioning handoff or turnover package without destination-owner acceptance and required artifact completeness.

domain_failure_modes: commissioning work is handed off without permit-card, approved-plan, temporary-utility, startup, or access evidence and the team arrives unable to start safely
test-readiness ownership is ambiguous across commissioning provider, controls, TAB, superintendent, and trade teams so the test window is missed
the lane marks work complete even though issues-log items, inspection objections, training records, systems-manual updates, or seasonal-testing obligations remain unresolved
schedule pressure causes the lane to imply permit approval, inspection signoff, owner acceptance, code interpretation, or licensed trade signoff that it does not own
a changed site-safety condition or stop-work notice is treated as routine scheduling noise and the wrong team proceeds with testing
tenant-specific commissioning, BAS, or PMIS routing is missing, so the lane guesses the next owner and drops evidence continuity

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local AHJ permit, inspection, occupancy, and certificate requirements vary materially by jurisdiction, project type, and tenant delivery model.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses OSHA, NYC DOB, WBDG, and USACE sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, permit type, tenant signoff rules, and local inspection sequence before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Commissioning provider scope, witness-signature rules, seasonal-testing obligations, and owner-acceptance thresholds may differ by tenant contract model.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline constrains the lane from assuming testing approval or owner acceptance authority. Runtime must retrieve tenant owner maps, commissioning procedures, and acceptance rules before routing execution-adjacent requests.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling instead of only generic coordination language.
The lane stops at coordination. Permit approval, inspection signoff, owner acceptance, code interpretation, licensed trade signoff, design judgment, and safety-critical release remain outside scope.
